help.helpMenuDescription: Below is a list of the options available for help.
help.helpMenuOptionSettingsDescription: Get help on how to configure this bot.
help.helpMenuOptionFormattingDescription: Get help on how to format your messages.
help.helpMenuOptionIdsDescription: Get help on how to use IDs. You can check users not inside your server or other server's info with this!
help.helpMenuOptionMsgVariablesDescription: Learn how to use message variables in your custom messages.
help.helpMenuOptionUnixTimeDescription: Learn what a unix timestamp is and how to use it.
help.helpEmbedFooter: If you couldn't find what you need, feel free to contact us through our official support server.
help.settingsHelpsContent:
Here are some common issues regarding how to configure the bot on your server, or just configuring your server in general.

**Q:** The random welcome message sent by Discord is still there after I enabled this bot's welcome feature!
**Ans:** Go to Server Settings -> Overview -> Find "SYSTEM MESSAGES CHANNEL" and change it as you like. If you are looking to disable the welcome message from this bot, use "/set-channel welcome" and keep the channel option empty.

**Q:** How do I limit members to only be able to use commands in channels I desire? / How do I limit members to only be able to use commands when they have certain roles? / How do I make exception for certain members?
**Ans:** Go to Server Settings -> Integrations -> Find Nanaz then you can configure however you like! (This settings page only exist on desktop! Mobile users **CANNOT** see it! If you insist to do it on mobile, go to a browser on your phone, then open Discord with desktop mode if your browser supports it.)

**Q:** I'm on mobile and everything is different! How do I do anything!?
**Ans:** We highly recommend you to set up the bot or your server on a desktop environment, however the name of the settings shouldn't change on mobile in most cases if you insists. Click on your avatar to find user settings, and instead of right clicking, hold your finger on what you want to find the options for to get the options to pop up. Some settings straight up doesn't exist, if you need them, you either need to just get on a PC or use Discord on a browser that supports desktop mode (such as Chrome) on your phone.
help.formattingHelpsContent:
Discord, and some other platforms, use **Markdown**, which is a system to create formatted texts.

Go to the bottom of the message if you only want to know how to put characters in a way like "\_Why is this not italic?\_"

Here are some syntaxs you can use!

"**Bold**": \*\*Bold\*\*
"_Italic_": \_Italic\_ or \*Italic\*
"__Underline__": \_\_Underline\_\_

# Heading 1
## Heading 2
### Heading 3
Equals to
\# Heading 1
\#\# Heading 2
\#\#\# Heading 3
respectively, note that the space is neccessary.

You can also put message
-# like this!
Original Message:
You can also put message
-\# like this!
Note that along with the headers, they need their own line and the space is neccessary.

[Here is a masked link that takes you to cat pictures.](https://www.google.com/search?q=cats&tbm=isch)
Original Message:
\[Here is a masked link that takes you to cat pictures.\](https://www.google.com/search?q=cats&tbm=isch)

- This is a list
- cats
  - cute
  - meow
* dogs
  * friend
  * woof

You can create this by putting "- " or "\* " in front of each line, remember that the space is neccessary. Add another 2 spaces at the very start to indent.

Original Message:

\- This is a list
\- cats
​ ​ \- cute
​ ​ \- meow
\* dogs
​ ​ \* friend
​ ​ \* woof

`This is a code block.`
Original Message:
\`This is a code block.\`
```
This is a cool code block.
I'm a line.
I'm not a line, wait I am!?```
Original Message:
\`\`\`This is a cool code block.
I'm a line.
I'm not a line, wait I am!?\`\`\`

If you are a programmer, you can also put the language name after the three "`" to make your code colorful like in your IDE, but you probably already knew this.

> I'm quoted
Original Message:
\> I'm quoted

> We' are quoted.
> I am.
> Me too.
Original Message:
\>\>\> We' are quoted.
I am.
Me too.

So, how did I put all these fancy characters without ruining everything? Well it's pretty simple, just put a "\\" before any character that has a special effect to "Escape" it!
Example: "\*\*I am escaped!\*\*"
Original Message: "\\​\*\\​\*I am escaped!\\​\*\\​\*"
help.idHelpsContent:
ID in Discord is a unique string of numbers assigned to every guild, user, channels, emojis and possibly more! Everything has their own ID, it's their only way of knowing each other!

To get something's ID, you'll need to turn on Developer Mode on Discord, head to your User Settings -> Advanced -> Turn on "Developer Mode".

Now you just have to right click on a user, a server, or anything, then click on "Copy _Something_ ID" to get their ID!
help.msgVariablesHelpsContent:
Message variables are special placeholders you can put in your custom messages that will be replaced with certain values when the message is sent. All variables are case-sensitive.

Here are some of the variables you can use:

`<[user]>` - Mentions the user.
`<[username]>` - The username of the user.
`<[userDisplayName]>` - The display name (nickname) of the user in the server, or username if no nickname is set.
`<[userId]>` - The ID of the user.
`<[serverName]>` - The name of the server.
`<[memberCount]>` - The total member count of the server.

Example usage in welcome message:
"Welcome to <[serverName]>, <[user]>! You are member number <[memberCount]>!"

This will be sent as: "Welcome to Cool Server, @NewUser! You are member number 150!"
help.unixTimeHelpsContent:
A Unix timestamp is a number that represents a date and time. It's the number of seconds since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC.

Example of use: "1643723400 = February 12, 2022, 14:30:00 UTC".

You can go to https://www.epochconverter.com/ to convert a date and time into a Unix timestamp.
